user_prompt:
Write one complete PHC Register Entry (Primary Health Centre) for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Kannada (code=kn, script=Kannada). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 46
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Tappers, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Shimoga
- first_language: Kannada
- clinical_persona_summary: A 46-year-old male from rural Karnataka, currently married, with an occupation as a toddy tapper and limited formal education. His primary language is Kannada, with functional Hindi and English. He lives in the Shimoga district and may have irregular attendance due to his focus on community and family responsibilities.

Document format guide for PHC Register Entry (Primary Health Centre) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC ...]] Village [[VILLAGE|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|19]] [[GENDER|Female]] | brief entry line

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre, Sira]] ಗ್ರಾಮ [[VILLAGE|Hirehalli]] ಜಿಲ್ಲೆ [[DISTRICT|Shimoga]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Gowda]] [[AGE|46]] [[GENDER|Male]] | ಶಂಕಿತ ಮಾರಕ ಗೆಡ್ಡೆಯ ದೀರ್ಘಕಾಲದ ಆರೈಕೆ ಅನುಸರಣೆ.

ರೋಗಿಯು ಗ್ರಾಮೀಣ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ 46 ವರ್ಷದ ಪುರುಷ, ವೃತ್ತಿಯಿಂದ ಮದ್ಯ ಸಂಗ್ರಹಕಾರ, 3 ತಿಂಗಳಿನಿಂದ ಕ್ರಮೇಣವಾಗಿ ನುಂಗಲು ಕಷ್ಟವಾಗುವುದು (dysphagia) ಮತ್ತು ಸುಮಾರು 8 ಕೆಜಿ ಅಚಾತುರ್ಯದ ತೂಕ ಇಳಿಕೆಯ ಇತಿಹಾಸವನ್ನು ಹೊಂದಿದ್ದಾರೆ. ಅವರು ಮಧ್ಯಂತರವಾಗಿ ನುಂಗುವಾಗ ನೋವು (odynophagia) ಮತ್ತು ನಿರಂತರವಾಗಿ ಒಣ ಕೆಮ್ಮು ಇರುವುದಾಗಿ ವರದಿ ಮಾಡಿದ್ದಾರೆ. ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಯ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ, ಅವರು ಕ್ಯಾಚೆಕ್ಟಿಕ್ ಆಗಿದ್ದು, BMI 17.5 ಆಗಿದೆ. ಎಡಭಾಗದಲ್ಲಿ 2x2 cm ಗಾತ್ರದ ಗಟ್ಟಿಯಾದ, ನೋವಿಲ್ಲದ ಕುತ್ತಿಗೆಯ ದುಗ್ಧರಸ ಗ್ರಂಥಿಯು (cervical lymph node) ಕಂಡುಬಂದಿದೆ. ರೋಗಿಯನ್ನು ಹೆಚ್ಚಿನ ಮೌಲ್ಯಮಾಪನಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ಜಿಲ್ಲಾ ಆಸ್ಪತ್ರೆಗೆ ಕಳುಹಿಸಲಾಗಿದೆ, ಇದರಲ್ಲಿ ಬೇರಿಯಮ್ ಸ್ವಾಲೋ ಮತ್ತು ಫೈನ್ ನೀಡಲ್ ಆಸ್ಪಿರೇಷನ್ ಸೈಟಾಲಜಿ ಸೇರಿವೆ.

[[PHONE_NUMBER|9880012345]]
[[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Shakuntala]]
[[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-KA-2023-456789]]
[[VOTER_ID|KA9876543210]]
[[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
generated_text_en:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre, Sira]] Village [[VILLAGE|Hirehalli]] District [[DISTRICT|Shimoga]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Gowda]] [[AGE|46]] [[GENDER|Male]] | Chronic care follow-up for suspected malignancy.

Patient is a 46-year-old male from rural Karnataka, occupation as a toddy tapper, presenting with a 3-month history of progressive dysphagia and unintentional weight loss of approximately 8 kilograms. He reports intermittent odynophagia and a persistent non-productive cough. On examination, he is cachectic with a BMI of 17.5. A palpable 2x2 cm firm, non-tender cervical lymph node is noted on the left side. The patient is referred to the District Hospital for further evaluation, including a barium swallow and fine needle aspiration cytology.

[[PHONE_NUMBER|9880012345]]
[[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Shakuntala]]
[[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-KA-2023-456789]]
[[VOTER_ID|KA9876543210]]
[[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
judge_reasoning: Kannada prose dominant and appropriate; all TYPEs allowed; persona/domain fit and surrogate details plausible with no script or length issues.

professional_persona: Mohammed Nasar, a 23‑year‑old driver, blends meticulous route planning and routine vehicle maintenance with a curiosity for local art, and they often share spontaneous smartphone photographs of roadside scenes while ensuring passengers reach their destinations safely and on time.
linguistic_persona: Mohammed Nasar is fluent in Malayalam, can read and write everyday texts, and possesses functional English sufficient to interpret traffic signs, GPS directions, and handle basic passenger requests, while having no formal knowledge of Hindi or other languages, and they occasionally practice listening to English tutorials online.
sports_persona: Mohammed Nasar, a cricket enthusiast, regularly plays matches at the village ground, follows the Kerala cricket team's Ranji Trophy games, and supports the Kerala Blasters in the Indian Super League, while occasionally joining friends for early‑morning jogs to stay fit.
arts_persona: Mohammed Nasar enjoys listening to soulful Mappila folk songs by K. J. Yesudas, watches Malayalam movies starring Mohanlal and Fahadh Faasil, and captures village life through his smartphone photography, inspired by the visual storytelling of cinematographer Santosh Sivan.
travel_persona: Mohammed Nasar prefers quick weekend getaways to nearby hill stations like Munnar and cultural towns such as Kozhikode, planning trips with GPS and a modest budget, while dreaming of exploring the historic forts of Hampi and the backwaters of Alleppey when his finances stabilize.
culinary_persona: Mohammed Nasar enjoys cooking Kerala biryani with aromatic basmati rice, ghee, and tender chicken, preparing soft appam using fermented rice batter and coconut milk, and relishes fish fry with raw mango chutney, often experimenting with mustard seeds and curry leaves for extra flavor.
persona: Mohammed Nasar, a curious 23‑year‑old driver, balances disciplined faith with a restless mind, often worrying over details yet finding calm in cricket, photography, and late‑night biryani cravings.
cultural_background: Mohammed Nasar is a 23‑year‑old Malayali Muslim from the rural outskirts of Malappuram, Kerala. He belongs to the Mappila community, which blends Arab‑influenced Islamic traditions with the vibrant Malayalam culture of South‑India. Growing up in a close‑knit village, he has been shaped by communal gatherings during Ramadan and Eid, as well as local festivals like Onam that celebrate harvest and art. His family environment emphasizes respect for elders, modesty, and a strong work ethic, which together with his curious nature give him an openness to new ideas and creative expressions.
linguistic_background: He grew up speaking Malayalam as his mother tongue and completed middle school education, which gave him functional literacy in Malayalam – he can read and write everyday texts, road signs and basic correspondence. For his driving job, he knows enough English to understand traffic signs, GPS instructions, and to communicate simple requests with occasional non‑Malayalam‑speaking passengers. He has no formal knowledge of Hindi or any other language, but his curiosity often leads him to listen to Malayalam songs and occasional YouTube tutorials in English.
skills_and_expertise: With several years behind the wheel, Mohammed has honed professional driving skills, including safe navigation of rural and urban roads, efficient route planning, and courteous customer interaction. He performs routine vehicle inspections, basic maintenance tasks like oil checks and tire pressure adjustments, and can troubleshoot minor mechanical issues. His familiarity with GPS and mobile payment apps ensures timely pickups and smooth cash handling. He also possesses good time‑management abilities, adapting to varying passenger schedules while maintaining reliability.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Professional Driving", "Safe Navigation of Rural and Urban Roads", "Route Planning", "Customer Service", "Vehicle Inspection", "Basic Vehicle Maintenance", "Minor Mechanical Troubleshooting", "GPS Navigation", "Mobile Payment Handling", "Time Management"]
hobbies_and_interests: Mohammed enjoys listening to traditional Mappila folk songs, watching Malayalam cinema, and playing cricket with friends in the village field. His curiosity about art leads him to capture roadside scenes with his smartphone camera and explore local craft fairs when they appear. He also likes cooking Kerala specialties like biryani and appam, and occasionally reads short stories in Malayalam to unwind after long shifts.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Mappila folk songs", "Watching Malayalam movies", "Playing cricket with friends", "Smartphone photography of roadside scenes", "Cooking Kerala dishes (biryani, appam)", "Reading Malayalam short stories", "Visiting local craft fairs"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Mohammed aspires to upgrade his livelihood by acquiring his own van and establishing a small transport service catering to local commuters and tourists in Malappuram. He aims to complete a certified driver‑training program to become an instructor, which would increase his earnings and give him a stable career path. Additionally, he wishes to save enough to support a future marriage and possibly pursue short courses in basic accounting and Malayalam literature, feeding his curiosity for art and learning while balancing his work responsibilities.
occupation: Car, Taxi and Van Drivers, Other
persona_clinical_summary: A 23-year-old male driver from rural Malappuram, Kerala, with a middle school education, works as a taxi driver. He is never married and speaks Malayalam as his first language. His lifestyle includes a disciplined routine, occasional concerns about details, and a preference for cricket and photography.

MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] എൻകൗണ്ടർ [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | കണ്ടെത്തലുകൾ: അറിയപ്പെടുന്ന ഒരു ഹെപ്പാറ്റിക് മാസ് (hepatic mass) വിലയിരുത്തുന്നതിനായി വയറിന്റെയും പെൽവിസിന്റെയും കോൺട്രാസ്റ്റ്-എൻഹാൻസ്ഡ് സിടി സ്കാൻ നടത്തി. ലിവർ വലത് ലോബിൽ ഏകദേശം 4.5 x 3.8 സെന്റിമീറ്റർ വലിപ്പമുള്ള ഒരു ഒറ്റപ്പെട്ട ലീഷൻ (solitary lesion) കാണിക്കുന്നു, ഇതിന് ആർട്ടീരിയൽ ഫേസിൽ ഹെറ്ററോജീനിയസ് എൻഹാൻസ്‌മെന്റും പോർട്ടൽ വീനസ് ഫേസിൽ വാഷ്ഔട്ടും ഉണ്ട്, ഇത് ഹെപ്പറ്റോസെല്ലുലാർ കാർസിനോമയുമായി (hepatocellular carcinoma) പൊരുത്തപ്പെടുന്നു. ഇൻട്രാ ഹെപ്പാറ്റിക് ബിലിയറി ഡൈലേഷൻ (intrahepatic biliary dilatation) അല്ലെങ്കിൽ വാസ്കുലർ ഇൻവേഷൻ (vascular invasion) എന്നിവയുടെ തെളിവുകളില്ല. പാൻക്രിയാസ്, പ്ലീഹ, രണ്ട് വൃക്കകൾ എന്നിവ സാധാരണ നിലയിലാണെന്ന് കാണപ്പെടുന്നു. ഫ്രീ ഫ്ലൂയിഡോ അസൈറ്റിസോ (free fluid or ascites) തിരിച്ചറിഞ്ഞിട്ടില്ല. അഡ്രീനൽ ഗ്രന്ഥികൾ വലിപ്പത്തിലും മോർഫോളജിയിലും സാധാരണമാണ്. കണ്ടെത്തലുകൾ കൂടുതൽ മാനേജ്‌മെന്റ് പ്ലാനിംഗിനായി റഫർ ചെയ്ത ഓങ്കോളജിസ്റ്റ് ഡോ. professional_persona: Lekshmi Manoj, a veteran community volunteer and household manager, leverages her meticulous budgeting, expert Kerala cooking, and handloom weaving skills to orchestrate temple festivals and mentor younger women, they balance practical organization with warm compassion.
linguistic_persona: Lekshmi Manoj, fluent in Malayalam with confident reading and writing, possesses a functional grasp of basic English for simple signage and forms, they primarily rely on Malayalam for all daily interactions.
sports_persona: Lekshmi Manoj, fond of early‑morning walks along the backwaters and occasional light swimming in the village pond, follows the Kerala cricket team's matches on TV and joins her local women’s yoga circle for gentle fitness, they never miss the festive cricket tournament arranged during Onam.
arts_persona: Lekshmi Manoj, a passionate devotee of classic Malayalam cinema, cherishes films starring Mohanlal and Mammootty, delights in the soulful renditions of K. J. Yesudas and K. S. Chithra, they regularly sing folk padams and verses from poet Vallathol, and attend local Kathakali performances whenever they tour her village.
travel_persona: Lekshmi Manoj, who prefers relaxed, family‑oriented journeys, enjoys seasonal houseboat cruises on Vembanad, occasional pilgrimages to nearby temple towns during festivals, and a memorable visit to Mysore's historic forts with her grandchildren, they always plan travel around communal holidays and modest budgets.
culinary_persona: Lekshmi Manoj, an accomplished Kerala cook, delights in preparing elaborate Sadhya feasts with dishes like avial, thoran, olan, and payasam, they favor the fragrant richness of coconut, turmeric, and curry leaves, prefer home‑cooked meals over eating out, and relish occasional visits to local toddy shops for fresh jackfruit churri.
persona: Lekshmi Manoj, a 64‑year‑old village matriarch, balances meticulous budgeting and traditional Kerala cooking with a warm, community‑focused spirit, they occasionally slip into late‑night television marathons of classic Malayalam movies.
cultural_background: Lekshmi Manoj is a 64-year-old Hindu Malayali woman living in the rural outskirts of Alappuzha, Kerala. She grew up in a joint-family environment where communal living, respect for elders, and close ties to the backwaters shaped her worldview. Her daily life is threaded with Kerala’s distinctive cultural threads – from cooking the traditional Kerala Sadhya during Onam, participating in local temple festivals, singing Malayalam folk songs, and hearing stories of boat-building traditions. The agrarian and fishing economy of her village has instilled a practical outlook, while the rich literary and performing arts of the region nurture her curiosity. Her cultural identity blends the simplicity of rural life with the vibrant customs of Malayali Hindu tradition.
linguistic_background: Malayalam is Lekshmi's mother tongue; she speaks it fluently, reads and writes the Malayalam script with confidence, and uses it for all daily communication. Her primary schooling (up to the primary level) gave her basic literacy in Malayalam and introductory exposure to English, enabling her to read simple English signs, fill basic forms, and understand spoken English in town markets, though her writing and speaking remain limited. She does not have formal knowledge of Hindi or any other language.
skills_and_expertise: Having spent a lifetime managing a household in a rural Kerala setting, Lekshmi possesses deep expertise in traditional Kerala cooking, including preparing elaborate Sadhya feasts, making appam, idiyappam, and various pickles. She is adept at budgeting household expenses, handling petty cash, and maintaining simple accounts. Her caregiving skills extend to raising children and caring for elderly family members with compassion. She actively volunteers in local temple committees, organizing festivals and coordinating community events. Additionally, she is skilled in handloom weaving and creating simple household textiles, and she can guide younger women in these crafts.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Traditional Kerala cooking', 'Household budgeting and finance management', 'Childcare and elder care', 'Community volunteer work and temple event organization', 'Handloom weaving and textile crafting', 'Mentoring younger women in traditional arts']
hobbies_and_interests: Lekshmi enjoys gardening, especially cultivating coconut and spice plants like turmeric and curry leaves. She loves singing traditional Malayalam folk songs (padams) during festivals, listening to classic Malayalam cinema, and attending temple festivals. She also spends time knitting simple shawls, reading devotional literature (Thirukural, Bhagavata), and taking leisurely walks along the backwaters with her family.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Gardening (coconut, turmeric, curry leaves)', 'Singing Malayalam folk songs', 'Watching classic Malayalam cinema', 'Attending temple festivals', 'Knitting shawls', 'Reading devotional literature (Thirukkural, Bhagavata Purana)', 'Walking along the backwaters']
career_goals_and_ambitions: At this stage, Lekshmi's ambitions are centered on preserving and sharing her cultural heritage. She wishes to compile a family cookbook of authentic Kerala recipes for her grandchildren, continue volunteering in the local temple committee, and mentor young women in traditional cooking and weaving. She also aims to maintain her health through regular yoga and walking, and to stay actively involved in community events, ensuring that the values of compassion, harmony, and practical wisdom are passed on.
persona_clinical_summary: A 64-year-old female from Kerala, currently married, with a primary education and Malayalam as her first language. She resides in a rural district, likely retired or a homemaker, and manages household budgeting while maintaining traditional cooking practices. Her lifestyle includes community-oriented activities and occasional television marathons of classic Malayalam films.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 52
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Buyers, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Mumbai Suburban
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 52-year-old female buyer with a matriculation education works in procurement in urban Mumbai, Maharashtra, and is currently married. She is a native Marathi speaker who is fluent in Hindi and leads a lifestyle that includes daily yoga and occasional consumption of festive sweets. Her professional persona is characterized by meticulous negotiation skills and methodical inventory control.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
District: [[DISTRICT|...]] | Chief complaint: <short clinical line>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ओपीडी स्लिप | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Sassoon General Hospital, Pune]]
रुग्ण: [[PATIENT_NAME|Sunita Patil]] | वय: [[AGE|52]] | लिंग: [[GENDER|Female]]
एमआरएन: [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] | डॉक्टर: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Vikram Deshmukh]]
जिल्हा: [[DISTRICT|Mumbai Suburban]] | मुख्य तक्रार: उजव्या बाजूला इंग्विनल हर्नियाचा त्रास 3 महिन्यांपासून, खोकल्यामुळे वाढतो.

वर्तमान आजाराचा इतिहास:
रुग्ण ही 52 वर्षांची महिला खरेदीदार आहे, जिला गेल्या तीन महिन्यांपासून उजव्या बाजूला कमी होणाऱ्या इंग्विनल सूजेचा इतिहास आहे. ती उजव्या जांघेत मंद वेदना होत असल्याचे सांगते, जी गेल्या महिन्यात अधिक जाणवू लागली आहे. जड वस्तू उचलणे आणि खोकल्यामुळे वेदना वाढतात. मळमळ, उलट्या किंवा शौचाच्या सवयींमध्ये बदल झाल्याचा कोणताही इतिहास नाही. तपासणीत सूज कमी होऊ शकते आणि तिला स्पर्श केल्यावर वेदना होत नाहीत.

तपासणी:
पोट: मऊ, वेदनाहीन, गार्डिंग किंवा रिजिडिटी नाही.
जांघ: उजव्या इंग्विनल भागात 4x3 सेमीची कमी होणारी सूज आहे, जी झोपल्यावर कमी होते. खोकल्याचा आवेग जाणवत नाही.

निदान:
उजवा इंग्विनल हर्निया, गुंतागुंत नसलेला.

योजना:
1. शस्त्रक्रिया दुरुस्तीची गरज रुग्णाला समजावून सांगणे.
2. ऐच्छिक उजव्या इंग्विनल हर्निया दुरुस्तीचे नियोजन करणे.
3. शस्त्रक्रियापूर्व रक्त तपासणी आणि ईसीजी करणे.
4. जड वस्तू उचलणे आणि ताण देणे टाळण्याचा सल्ला देणे.

फॉलो-अप:
रुग्णाला शस्त्रक्रियापूर्व मूल्यांकनासाठी एका आठवड्यात परत येण्याचा सल्ला दिला.

संपर्क: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9123456789]]
आणीबाणी संपर्क: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Patil]]
कर्मचारी आयडी: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-MH-8842]]
जन्म तारीख: [[DOB|1972-05-20]]
व्यवसाय: [[OCCUPATION|Buyer, Other]]
रुग्णालय आयडी: [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-MH-001]]

District: [[DISTRICT|Mumbai Suburban]] | Chief complaint: Right-sided inguinal hernia pain for 3 months, worsening with cough.

History of Present Illness:
The patient is a 52-year-old female buyer who presents with a history of a reducible right-sided inguinal swelling for the past three months. She reports a dull ache in the right groin, which has become more prominent over the last month. The pain is exacerbated by lifting heavy objects and coughing. There is no history of nausea, vomiting, or change in bowel habits. The swelling is reducible and non-tender on examination.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, no guarding or rigidity.
Groin: A 4x3 cm reducible swelling is present in the right inguinal region, which is reducible on lying down. No cough impulse is felt.

Diagnosis:
Right inguinal hernia, uncomplicated.

Plan:
1. Counsel patient regarding the need for surgical repair.
2. Schedule for elective right inguinal hernia repair.
3. Pre-operative blood work and ECG to be done.
4. Advise to avoid heavy lifting and straining.

Follow-up:
Patient advised to return in one week for pre-operative assessment.

user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 25
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Tester
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Pune
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 25-year-old male from Pune, Maharashtra, who is never married and works as a software tester. He holds a Higher Secondary education and speaks Marathi as his first language, with a strong command of Hindi and English. His lifestyle includes a preference for urban living, regular weekend cricket, and a diet rich in Pune’s street foods like vada pav and misal pav. He is methodical and detail-oriented but tends to procrastinate on health check-ups.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: <clinical prose>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
शस्त्रक्रिया नोंद — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Ruby Hall Clinic]] [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-001]] [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Rohan Patil]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Vikram Deshmukh]]
प्रक्रिया / निष्कर्ष: रुग्ण पुणे येथील 25 वर्षांचा पुरुष सॉफ्टवेअर टेस्टर आहे, ज्याला ओपन अपेंडेक्टोमीसाठी दाखल करण्यात आले होते. शस्त्रक्रियेच्या दिवशी सकाळी रुग्णाला ऑपरेशन थिएटरमध्ये नेण्यात आले. जनरल ॲनेस्थेशिया कोणत्याही गुंतागुंतीशिवाय देण्यात आला. उजव्या खालच्या चतुर्थांशात एक मानक मॅकबर्नी छेद देण्यात आला. अपेंडिक्समध्ये सूज, सूज आणि टोकाकडून छिद्र असल्याचे आढळले. संपूर्ण पेरिटोनियल वॉशआउट करण्यात आले. अपेंडिक्स काढून टाकले आणि हिस्टोपॅथॉलॉजीसाठी पाठवले. जखम थरांमध्ये बंद करण्यात आली. रुग्णाने प्रक्रिया चांगल्या प्रकारे सहन केली आणि त्याला पोस्ट-ऑपरेटिव्ह वॉर्डमध्ये हलवण्यात आले.
[[BED_NUMBER|12]] [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-78945]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sunita Patil]]
generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Ruby Hall Clinic]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-001]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Rohan Patil]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Vikram Deshmukh]]
Procedure / findings: Patient is a 25-year-old male software tester from Pune, admitted for an open appendectomy. The patient was taken to the operating theatre on the morning of the day of surgery. General anesthesia was induced without any complications. A standard McBurney incision was made in the right lower quadrant. The appendix was found to be inflamed, edematous, and perforated at the tip. A thorough peritoneal washout was performed. The appendix was resected and sent for histopathology. The wound was closed in layers. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was shifted to the post-operative ward.
[[BED_NUMBER|12]] [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-78945]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sunita Patil]]
judge_reasoning: Marathi Devanagari prose matches expected language; persona (male, 25, Pune) fits surgical content; all entity TYPEs allowed; medical terms and Latin tags acceptable per rules; no mismatches or violations.

professional_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old salt‑pan mukadam, leverages their practical, structured mindset and strong oral negotiation skills to manage labor, control evaporation cycles, and secure fair prices, while their outgoing nature keeps the team cohesive despite occasional bouts of anxiety.
linguistic_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old village dweller, speaks Punjabi fluently for daily life, understands spoken Hindi enough for casual chats, but cannot read or write in either language, relying on their strong oral storytelling and negotiation abilities.
sports_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old village cricket enthusiast, plays weekly matches on the local ground, follows the Punjab Kings in the IPL, and stays fit with regular morning jogs, though occasional late‑night bhangra sessions sometimes cut into their recovery time.
arts_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old lover of Punjabi folk, often sings Gurdas Maan’s classics, dances bhangra to Diljit Dosanjh’s beats, watches Bollywood blockbusters starring Shah Rukh Khan and Alia Bhatt on the community TV, and participates in nightly storytelling circles that keep local folklore alive.
travel_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old who enjoys short weekend getaways, often visits a nearby town’s mela for shopping and street food, dreams of a pilgrimage to Varanasi for spiritual renewal, and hopes to take a group bus trip with friends once they can afford it.
culinary_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old who loves rustic cuisine, enjoys preparing makki di roti with sarson da saag seasoned with mustard seeds, ginger, and fresh spinach, relishes buttery lassi and spicy pickles made from raw mango, and often trades recipes during village gatherings.
persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old salt‑pan foreman, blends practical structure with lively village camaraderie, but their frequent anxiety and occasional impulsive splurges on sweets keep life both organized and unpredictably sweet.
cultural_background: Gurmej Kumar belongs to a close‑knit Punjabi rural community in the Bathinda district of Punjab. His family has traditionally engaged in salt‑pan work, a livelihood shaped by the arid landscape of the region. The social fabric is woven around joint‑family values, seasonal festivals like Baisakhi and Lohri, and a strong sense of community responsibility. Living in a village setting, he participates in local gatherings, fairs (melas), and agricultural cycles that define daily life.
linguistic_background: His mother tongue is Punjabi, which he speaks fluently and uses for all daily communication. He has limited exposure to Hindi, primarily understanding it in oral interactions but cannot read or write either language due to his illiterate status. While he lacks formal literacy, he possesses strong oral communication skills, enabling him to convey instructions and negotiate effectively in his role as a mukadam.
skills_and_expertise: As a mukadam in salt production, Gurmej manages a team of laborers, oversees the evaporation and crystallisation processes, and ensures the maintenance of salt pans and related equipment. He has practical knowledge of water‑flow control, timing of sun exposure, and quality assessment of harvested salt. His role also involves oral bookkeeping, negotiating prices with buyers, coordinating with village authorities, and resolving worker disputes. His sociable nature enhances his ability to motivate and organize groups efficiently.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Supervising salt-pan laborers", "Overseeing salt evaporation and crystallisation", "Maintaining salt-pan infrastructure", "Managing water-flow and drying schedules", "Assessing salt quality", "Oral bookkeeping and price negotiation", "Coordinating with village authorities", "Resolving worker disputes", "Motivating teams through group activities"]
hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys participating in village cricket matches, singing Punjabi folk songs, and dancing bhangra during festivals. Gurmej often attends local melas and fairs, helps organize community events, and likes cooking traditional Punjabi dishes like makki di roti and sarson da saag. In his spare time, he watches Bollywood movies on a community television set and engages in informal storytelling sessions with friends.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Playing cricket in the village", "Singing Punjabi folk songs", "Dancing bhangra at festivals", "Attending local melas and fairs", "Cooking traditional Punjabi dishes", "Watching Bollywood movies on community TV", "Participating in storytelling circles with friends"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Despite being illiterate, Gurmej aspires to advance within the salt‑production sector by gaining practical expertise that could eventually lead to owning a small salt‑pan of his own. He aims to improve his family's economic stability, provide better education opportunities for his children, and learn basic reading‑writing skills through community adult‑literacy programs. In the long term, he hopes to become a respected community leader who can represent workers’ interests in local cooperatives.
persona_clinical_summary: A 25-year-old male from Bathinda, Punjab, currently married, works as a salt-pan mukadam with an illiterate education level in a rural zone. His primary language is Punjabi, and he lives in a close-knit rural community. Clinically, he exhibits frequent anxiety and impulsive behaviors, such as overeating sweets.

linguistic_persona: Preeti Kaur, a fluent Punjabi speaker who relies on oral communication, understands spoken Hindi well enough for simple chats, but cannot read or write in any script, navigating daily life through memory, listening, and a practical skepticism toward written instructions.
sports_persona: Preeti Kaur enjoys walking to the choupal for daily gossip, cheerfully supports the Punjab Kings in IPL and follows Indian cricket matches, and occasionally joins the village’s women's walking group and watches local kabaddi tournaments on TV, staying moderately active despite a preference for relaxed routine.
arts_persona: Preeti Kaur cherishes listening to Punjabi folk legends like Gurdas Maan and Surinder Kaur, dances enthusiastically to bhangra beats, knits scarves while humming boliyan, preserves the art of phulkari embroidery, and delights in reciting age-old Punjabi proverbs that color her conversations.
travel_persona: Preeti Kaur prefers short train trips to nearby spiritual hubs like Amritsar and Rishikesh and occasional weekend getaways to hill stations such as Shimla, planning spontaneously with family while balancing modest finances and a love for exploring new scenery.
culinary_persona: Preeti Kaur relishes preparing sarson da saag with makki di roti, dal makhni, and traditional sweets, skillfully cultivates bitter gourd and coriander in her kitchen garden, and favors cooking with iron tawa and wooden spatulas, shunning modern electric appliances despite occasional curiosity.
persona: Preeti Kaur, a 55-year-old culinary and craft guardian, blends traditional Punjabi flavors and phulkari art with a skeptical, emotionally intense streak that flares up when modern shortcuts or music cuts off unexpectedly.
cultural_background: Preeti Kaur hails from a Punjabi Sikh family rooted in the agricultural rhythms of Muktsar, a rural town in the heart of Punjab. Her upbringing is steeped in vibrant folk traditions—Baisakhi celebrations, Punjabi folk songs, and the Sikh values of seva, humility, and community solidarity. The daily life of her village, with its fields, cattle, and extended families, has shaped her practical outlook while also leaving room for her curious mind to explore new household strategies.
linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Punjabi, which she speaks fluently and uses for all daily interactions. Because she never attended school, she is illiterate in both the Gurmukhi script used for Punjabi and the Devanagari script for Hindi; she cannot read or write in any language. She can understand spoken Hindi well enough to follow simple instructions and chat with neighbours, but all her communication is oral.
skills_and_expertise: Through decades of managing her home, Preeti has honed a range of practical skills. She excels at preparing traditional Punjabi dishes—such as sarson da saag, makki di roti, dal makhni, and festive sweets—for family and communal meals. She is a skilled handloom phulkari embroiderer, creating vibrant floral motifs on cloths used in weddings and festivals. She maintains a modest kitchen garden that supplies seasonal vegetables, and she looks after a small flock of dairy goats, providing fresh milk for daily use. She is proficient in leading oral recitations of Gurbani during family prayers and can guide the performance of Sikh rites despite her illiteracy. Additionally, she efficiently handles cash‑based household budgeting and is comfortable organising Langar for community gatherings. She is skeptical of modern kitchen appliances, preferring traditional wooden tools and the proven techniques of her ancestors, and she can become emotionally reactive if a cooking mishap leads to burnt food.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Traditional Punjabi cooking", "Phulkari (handloom embroidery)", "Small‑scale vegetable gardening", "Dairy goat husbandry", "Sikh devotional singing (Kirtan)", "Cash‑based household budgeting", "Organising Langar events"]
hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys listening to Punjabi folk music such as bhangra beats and boliyan, especially during evenings on the radio. She likes knitting scarves and small shawls for the winter months. She spends time maintaining her kitchen garden, growing seasonal vegetables like bitter gourd and coriander. Attending Gurdwara services and participating in community Langar as a volunteer bring her spiritual satisfaction. She also loves taking part in village kite‑flying festivals during the windy winter, collecting and reciting old Punjabi proverbs, and strolling to the choupal for casual gossip with neighbours. When her favorite bhangra track ends abruptly, she can feel a surge of disappointment, reflecting her high emotional reactivity.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Punjabi folk music", "Knit scarves", "Maintaining a kitchen garden", "Attending Gurdwara services", "Participating in village kite‑flying festivals", "Collecting and reciting Punjabi proverbs", "Walking to the choupal for gossip"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Preeti Kaur wishes to ensure that the rich culinary and craft traditions of her Punjabi Sikh heritage are passed down to the next generation. She hopes to mentor her grandchildren and other village girls in preparing classic Punjabi dishes and creating phulkari work, perhaps through informal weekend workshops held at her home. She also aspires to start a modest home‑based venture selling homemade pickles, traditional sweets, and embroidered items at the weekly market, providing a small but steady supplementary income. Recognising her anxiety, she aims to deepen her spiritual practice—regular meditation on Gurbani and increased seva at the local Gurdwara—to help manage emotional stress. While she prefers flexible, unstructured schedules, she is keen to see her family and community benefit from her practical knowledge.
persona_clinical_summary: A 55-year-old female from rural Punjab, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiterate education. She speaks Punjabi as her first language and has limited literacy in Gurmukhi or Devanagari. Her lifestyle includes traditional cooking and phulkari embroidery, with a skeptical temperament.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

linguistic_persona: Esther Mary is a native Tamil speaker; they read and write fluently in Tamil, possess functional English for workplace instructions, and can recognize a handful of Hindi words from occasional media exposure.
sports_persona: Esther Mary is an avid cricket fan who regularly watches matches, especially supporting the Chennai Super Kings, and enjoys occasional morning walks along the coastal promenade, though they rarely participate in organized sports.
arts_persona: Esther Mary engages with the arts by singing devotional hymns in the church choir, enjoying Tamil cinema featuring actors Vijay and Nayanthara, listening to A.R. Rahman’s melodies, and reading novels by author Sujatha, which together enrich their cultural outlook.
travel_persona: Esther Mary prefers short road trips to nearby coastal towns like Kanyakumari and historic sites such as the Mahabalipuram temples, often planning family outings with a modest budget, while dreaming of a future visit to Sri Lanka’s cultural heartland.

persona: Esther Mary, a 38‑year‑old abrasive‑slitting specialist, blends meticulous craftsmanship with a love for community choir, Tamil vegetarian cooking, and cricket, while juggling ambitious career goals, prudent finances, and a habit of over‑stocking spices in their kitchen.
cultural_background: Esther Mary is part of the Tamil Christian community in Thoothukkudi, a coastal town in Tamil Nadu. The region blends a traditional agrarian and fishing lifestyle with a modest industrial presence where she works. Her family observes Tamil cultural practices such as Pongal, respect for elders, and community gatherings, while also celebrating Christian festivals like Christmas, Easter, and Sunday worship. Growing up in a rural setting, she internalises the collective values of modesty, hard work, and hospitality, infused with the occasional exposure to modern manufacturing influences.
linguistic_background: Esther’s mother tongue is Tamil, which she reads, writes, and speaks fluently. While she has limited formal training in English, she can understand simple workplace instructions in English and communicate essential information with her coworkers. She has no formal knowledge of Hindi, but occasional exposure to Hindi media in the region allows her to recognize a few common words.
skills_and_expertise: Esther is a proficient operator of abrasive slitting machines, adept at setting up the equipment, adjusting feed rates, and monitoring blade wear. Her meticulous attention to detail ensures high‑quality output with minimal defects. career_goals_and_ambitions: Esther aims to deepen her technical knowledge by enrolling in short‑term training programmes on advanced abrasive slitting technology, safety management, and lean manufacturing. system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

செயல்முறை / கண்டறிதல்கள்: நோயாளி 38 வயது பெண், தூத்துக்குடியைச் சேர்ந்தவர், தமிழ்நாட்டைச் சேர்ந்தவர். கடந்த ஆறு மாதங்களாக வலது கீழ் வயிற்றுப் பகுதியில் நாள்பட்ட வலியால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளார். கடுமையான வலி, குமட்டல் மற்றும் வாந்தி ஆகியவற்றுடன் நோயாளி வந்தார். பரிசோதனையில், வலது இலியாக் ஃபோசா பகுதியில் மென்மை மற்றும் தசை இறுக்கம் காணப்பட்டது. அல்ட்ராசவுண்ட் உள்ளிட்ட ஆய்வுகளில் 6.5 செ.மீ. அப்பெண்டிசியல் மாஸ் கண்டறியப்பட்டது. நோயாளிக்கு லேப்ராஸ்கோபிக் அப்பெண்டிக்டமி செய்யப்பட்டது. பொது மயக்க மருந்து அளிக்கப்பட்ட நிலையில், நோயாளி மல்லாக்கப் படுக்க வைக்கப்பட்டது. தொப்புளில் 10 மி.மீ. ட்ரோக்கர் மூலம் நியூமோபெரிட்டோனியம் உருவாக்கப்பட்டது. இடது கீழ் கால்பகுதி மற்றும் சூப்ராபியூபிக் பகுதியில் இரண்டு 5 மி.மீ. போர்ட்கள் வைக்கப்பட்டன. அப்பெண்டிஸ் வீக்கமடைந்து, ஓமெண்டத்துடன் ஒட்டியிருந்தது கண்டறியப்பட்டது. அப்பெண்டிஸ் பிரிக்கப்பட்டு, எண்டோலூப்கள் மூலம் கட்டப்பட்டு, எண்டோபேக்கில் அகற்றப்பட்டது. ஸ்டம்ப் ஒரு எண்டோலூப் மூலம் தலைகீழாக மாற்றப்பட்டது. ஓமெண்டம் அப்பெண்டிஸிலிருந்து பிரிக்கப்பட்டது. பெரிட்டோனியல் குழி நார்மல் சலைன் கொண்டு கழுவப்பட்டது. வயிற்றுப் பகுதியில் வேறு எந்த நோயியல் பாதிப்புகளும் கண்டறியப்படவில்லை. போர்ட்கள் அகற்றப்பட்டன, 10 மி.மீ. போர்ட் இடத்தில் உள்ள ஃபாசியா விக்கிரி 0 மூலம் மூடப்பட்டது. தோல் சப்-குட்டிகுலர் தையல்கள் மூலம் மூடப்பட்டது. நோயாளி செயல்முறையை நன்றாக ஏற்றுக்கொண்டார். மதிப்பிடப்பட்ட இரத்த இழப்பு மிகக் குறைவு. நோயாளி நிலையான நிலையில் அறுவை சிகிச்சைக்குப் பிந்தைய வார்டுக்கு மாற்றப்பட்டார். generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital, Thoothukkudi]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1123]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]] [[AGE|38]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Aravind Kumar]]
Procedure / findings: The patient is a 38-year-old female from Thoothukkudi, Tamil Nadu, with a history of chronic right lower quadrant pain for the past six months. She presented with acute onset of severe pain, nausea, and vomiting. On examination, she was tender in the right iliac fossa with guarding. Investigations including ultrasound showed a 6.5 cm appendiceal mass. The patient was taken up for laparoscopic appendectomy. Under general anesthesia, the patient was positioned in the supine position. Pneumoperitoneum was created using a 10 mm trocar at the umbilicus. Two 5 mm ports were placed in the left lower quadrant and suprapubic region. The appendix was found to be inflamed and adherent to the omentum. The appendix was dissected, ligated with endoloops, and removed in an endobag. The stump was inverted with a single endoloop. The omentum was separated from the appendix. The peritoneal cavity was irrigated with normal saline. No other intra-abdominal pathology was noted. The ports were removed, and the fascia at the 10 mm port site was closed with Vicryl 0. The skin was closed with subcuticular sutures. The patient tolerated the procedure well. Estimated blood loss was minimal. The patient was shifted to the post-operative ward in a stable condition. [[BED_NUMBER|12]] [[MRN|MRN-2024-1123-001]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-THOOT-7890]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]

She is a retired homemaker managing household budgeting and traditional cooking. Her lifestyle includes daily yoga and community festival coordination, with limited English proficiency.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):

Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]

generated_text:
அறுவை சிகிச்சை குறிப்பு — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Erode District Medical College Hospital]] அனுமதி [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-001]] வார்டு [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Lakshmi]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]] அறுவை சிகிச்சை நிபுணர் [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Ramesh Kumar]]
செயல்முறை / கண்டறிதல்கள்: நோயாளி 45 வயதுடைய, ஊரக எரோடு, தமிழ்நாடு பகுதியைச் சேர்ந்த இல்லத்தரசி ஆவார். சிக்கலான வலது பக்க மார்பகக் கட்டி காரணமாக அனுமதிக்கப்பட்டார். மேமோகிராபி மற்றும் கோர் நீடில் பயாப்ஸி உள்ளிட்ட அறுவை சிகிச்சைக்கு முந்தைய பரிசோதனைகள், 5.2 செ.மீ. அளவுள்ள, இரண்டாம் நிலை தரம் கொண்ட இன்வேசிவ் டக்டல் கார்சினோமாவை உறுதிப்படுத்தின. நோயாளி 15 ஆகஸ்ட் 2024 அன்று அறுவை சிகிச்சை அறைக்கு அழைத்துச் செல்லப்பட்டார். அக்குள் நிணநீர் முடிச்சு அகற்றுதலுடன் கூடிய மாற்றியமைக்கப்பட்ட ரேடிக்கல் மாஸ்டெக்டோமி செய்யப்பட்டது. சென்டினல் முடிச்சுகளின் அறுவை சிகிச்சையின் போது எடுக்கப்பட்ட உறைந்த திசுப் பரிசோதனை (frozen section) எதிர்மறையாக இருந்தது. திசு நோயியல் பரிசோதனைக்காக மாதிரி அனுப்பப்பட்டது. நோயாளி செயல்முறையை நன்கு ஏற்றுக்கொண்டார் மற்றும் அறுவை சிகிச்சைக்குப் பிறகு அறுவை சிகிச்சை வார்டுக்கு மாற்றப்பட்டார். அவரது [[PATIENT_ID|PID-ERD-2024-0815]] மற்றும் [[MRN|MRN-ERD-2024-0815-001]] பதிவு செய்யப்பட்டுள்ளன. அவரது கணவர், [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ponnusamy]], செயல்முறை மற்றும் அறுவை சிகிச்சைக்குப் பிந்தைய பராமரிப்புத் திட்டம் குறித்துத் தெரிவிக்கப்பட்டது. அவர் அறுவை சிகிச்சைக்குப் பிந்தைய வார்டில் [[BED_NUMBER|12]] நிலையில் வைக்கப்பட்டார்.
generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Erode District Medical College Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-001]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Lakshmi]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Ramesh Kumar]]
Procedure / findings: Patient is a 45-year-old female homemaker from rural Erode, Tamil Nadu, admitted for a complex right-sided breast mass. Pre-operative workup including mammography and core needle biopsy confirmed a 5.2 cm invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 2. The patient was taken to the operating theatre on 15 August 2024. A modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection was performed. Intraoperative frozen section of the sentinel nodes was negative. The specimen was sent for histopathology. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was shifted to the surgical ward post-op. Her [[PATIENT_ID|PID-ERD-2024-0815]] and [[MRN|MRN-ERD-2024-0815-001]] are recorded. Her husband, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ponnusamy]], was informed of the procedure and post-operative care plan. She was placed in [[BED_NUMBER|12]] in the post-operative ward.
judge_reasoning: Tamil-script prose matches expected language; oncology content fits female 45yo persona; all TYPEs from allow-list; Latin values inside tags permitted; no mismatches or violations.

professional_persona: Neelakanteswarara Pasagada, a 32‑year‑old railway track maintenance technician, excels at geometry assessments, welding, and operating heavy tamping machinery, while they use their imaginative mind to devise quick, cost‑effective fixes using locally sourced components.
linguistic_persona: Neelakanteswarara Pasagada speaks Telugu fluently for daily communication, reads and writes it well, possesses functional Hindi for basic instructions and forms, and they rely on railway‑specific English jargon for oral exchanges on the job.
sports_persona: Neelakanteswarara Pasagada regularly joins village cricket matches with the Sri Potti Sriramulu Nellore Cricket Club, follows the Indian Premier League team Royal Challengers Bangalore, and they maintain basic fitness through early morning jogs, though they often skip formal gym sessions due to work schedules.
arts_persona: Neelakanteswarara Pasagada indulges in sketching rural landscapes, reads science‑fiction comics and mythological graphic novels, enjoys folk dance performances during Bathukamma, and they admire Indian painter M. F. Husain and playback singer S. P. Balasubrahmanyam.
travel_persona: Neelakanteswarara Pasagada prefers road trips to nearby hill stations like Araku Valley and coastal retreats such as Visakhapatnam during festival breaks, plans trips around long weekends, and they dream of trekking in the Himalayas but wait for a raise to afford the journey.
culinary_persona: Neelakanteswarara Pasagada enjoys spicy millet porridge (ragi mudde) with gongura chutney, relishes tamarind‑flavored pulihora, tender chicken biryani, and sweet jalebi during festivals, but rarely cooks at home, and they prefer to savor meals at the local dhaba.
persona: Neelakanteswarara Pasagada is a 32‑year‑old railway track technician whose knack for frugal, quick fixes blends with a vivid imagination for sketches and sci‑fi comics, and they balance energetic village festivals with a competitive edge that fuels skepticism toward easy shortcuts.
cultural_background: Born and raised in the rural town of Sri Potti Sriramulu Nellore in Andhra Pradesh, Neelakanteswarara belongs to a Telugu-speaking agrarian community. His family follows traditional customs like celebrating Ugadi, Sankranti, and Bathukamma with communal feasts and folk dances. The close-knit village life, with frequent temple gatherings, railway workshops, and cooperative farming, has shaped his sense of belonging and respect for regional heritage. He also grew up around the railway tracks, witnessing the rhythm of trains that sparked his fascination with mechanics.
linguistic_background: Neelakanteswarara's mother tongue is Telugu; he reads and writes Telugu fluently and uses it for daily communication at home and in the village. His primary education gave him basic literacy in Hindi and English, enabling him to understand simple signs, railway manuals and fill out work forms, though his proficiency remains limited to functional usage. He can converse in Hindi at a rudimentary level and read basic instructions, while his English is mostly oral and confined to railway jargon.
skills_and_expertise: Neelakanteswarara is proficient in rail track maintenance, including track geometry assessment, welding, and metal fabrication. He operates heavy machinery such as track tampers and ballast regulators, follows safety protocols, and conducts routine inspections. His imaginative mind allows him to devise quick fixes using available resources, and he is adept at problem solving under pressure. Additionally, he has basic artistic skills in sketching, which he uses to plan repairs.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Rail track maintenance", "Track geometry assessment", "Welding", "Metal fabrication", "Heavy machinery operation", "Safety compliance", "Problem solving", "Sketching for planning"]
hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys group activities like village cricket matches and folk dance gatherings, which satisfy his sociable nature. His imaginative side draws him to sketching, painting simple rural landscapes, and exploring abstract concepts through reading mythology and science fiction comics. He loves stargazing on clear nights, tinkering with small mechanical gadgets, and participating in community festivals such as Ugadi and Bathukamma.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Cricket matches", "Folk dance gatherings", "Sketching", "Painting rural landscapes", "Reading mythology", "Science fiction comics", "Stargazing", "Tinkering with mechanical gadgets", "Participating in festivals (Ugadi, Bathukamma)"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: To become a senior track supervisor, obtain formal certification through Indian Railways' training programs, and introduce innovative, cost-effective methods for track maintenance using locally sourced materials. He aspires to lead a team, improve safety standards, and eventually mentor younger workers. Long‑term, he hopes to start a small workshop that combines his technical expertise with his artistic inclination, perhaps designing decorative rail signage or functional art installations.
persona_clinical_summary: A 32‑year‑old male from rural Andhra Pradesh, never married, with primary education, works as a railway track repairer. He speaks Telugu and has basic literacy in Hindi and English. Clinically, he may present with occupational hazards from track work and limited access to preventive healthcare in his rural district. His lifestyle includes participation in village festivals and occasional cricket, with a diet rich in local grains and lentils.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER
